input.prompt:
I'm upgrading a .NET 9 app to .NET 10. It:
- Reads SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm, SslStream.CipherAlgorithm, SslStream.HashAlgorithm, and their strength properties for TLS connection logging
- Registers a callback on SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown for cleanup
- Uses Queryable.MaxBy with an IComparer<TSource> overload
- Has XSLT transforms with XsltSettings.EnableScript = true
What warnings or errors will I get?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about breaking changes and warnings when upgrading from .NET 9 to .NET 10 for specific APIs they're using. Let me research what's changed in .NET 10 for these specific APIs.

Let me think about what I know about .NET 10 breaking changes for these specific APIs:

1. **SslStream properties (KeyExchangeAlgorithm, CipherAlgorithm, HashAlgorithm, and strength properties)**: In .NET 10, these properties on SslStream were obsoleted. They were marked with `[Obsolete]` attributes because TLS 1.3 doesn't expose individual algorithm details the same way. The properties like `CipherAlgorithm`, `CipherStrength`, `HashAlgorithm`, `HashStrength`, `KeyExchangeAlgorithm`, `KeyExchangeStrength` were obsoleted with specific diagnostic IDs (SYSLIB0058 I believe, or similar).


2. **SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown**: The `Microsoft.Win32.SystemEvents` members were obsoleted in .NET 10 due to platform compatibility issues and threading problems, so the `EventsThreadShutdown` event is now marked as obsolete.
3. **Queryable.MaxBy with IComparer<TSource> overload**: I'm trying to recall the specifics here—I believe the `Queryable.MaxBy` and `MinBy` overloads that accept an `IComparer` parameter were either removed or had their signatures changed in .NET 10.
4. **XsltSettings.EnableScript = true**: XSLT script execution has been restricted across various .NET versions, and in .NET 10, `XsltSettings.EnableScript` was marked obsolete with script execution potentially throwing by default.

I should verify these details against the official .NET 10 breaking changes documentation to provide accurate diagnostic information.

Starting in .NET 5, some APIs that are newly marked as obsolete make use of two new properties on [ObsoleteAttribute](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.obsoleteattribute).

*   The [ObsoleteAttribute.DiagnosticId](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.obsoleteattribute.diagnosticid#system-obsoleteattribute-diagnosticid) property tells the compiler to generate build warnings using a custom diagnostic ID. The custom ID allows for obsoletion warning to be suppressed specifically and separately from one another. In the case of the `System*` namespace obsoletions, the format for the custom diagnostic ID is `SYSLIB0XXX`. In the case of the `Microsoft.Extensions` obsoletions, the format for the custom diagnostic ID is `EXTOBS0XXX`.
*   The [ObsoleteAttribute.UrlFormat](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.obsoleteattribute.urlformat#system-obsoleteattribute-urlformat) property tells the compiler to include a URL link to learn more about the obsoletion.

If you encounter build warnings or errors due to usage of an obsolete API, follow the specific guidance provided for the diagnostic ID listed in the reference tables that follow. Warnings or errors for these obsoletions _can't_ be suppressed using the [standard diagnostic ID (CS0618)](../../csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0618) for obsolete types or members; use the custom `SYSLIB0XXX` or `EXTOBS0XXX` diagnostic ID values instead. For more information, see [Suppress warnings](#suppress-warnings).

The following tables provide an index to obsolete APIs with custom diagnostic IDs in .NET 5 and later versions:

*   [SYSLIB obsoletions](#syslib-obsoletions)
*   [EXTOBS obsoletions](#extobs-obsoletions)

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/obsoletions-overview:
Starting in .NET 5, some APIs that are newly marked as obsolete make use of two new properties on [ObsoleteAttribute](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.obsoleteattribute).

*   The [ObsoleteAttribute.DiagnosticId](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.obsoleteattribute.diagnosticid#system-obsoleteattribute-diagnosticid) property tells the compiler to generate build warnings using a custom diagnostic ID. The custom ID allows for obsoletion warning to be suppressed specifically and separately from one another. In the case of the `System*` namespace obsoletions, the format for the custom diagnostic ID is `SYSLIB0XXX`. In the case of the `Microsoft.Extensions` obsoletions, the format for the custom diagnostic ID is `EXTOBS0XXX`.
*   The [ObsoleteAttribute.UrlFormat](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.obsoleteattribute.urlformat#system-obsoleteattribute-urlformat) property tells the compiler to include a URL link to learn more about the obsoletion.

If you encounter build warnings or errors due to usage of an obsolete API, follow the specific guidance provided for the diagnostic ID listed in the reference tables that follow. Warnings or errors for these obsoletions _can't_ be suppressed using the [standard diagnostic ID (CS0618)](../../csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0618) for obsolete types or members; use the custom `SYSLIB0XXX` or `EXTOBS0XXX` diagnostic ID values instead. For more information, see [Suppress warnings](#suppress-warnings).

The following tables provide an index to obsolete APIs with custom diagnostic IDs in .NET 5 and later versions:

*   [SYSLIB obsoletions](#syslib-obsoletions)
*   [EXTOBS obsoletions](#extobs-obsoletions)

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/10.0:
If you're migrating an app to .NET 10, the breaking changes listed here might affect you. Changes are grouped by technology area, such as ASP.NET Core or Windows Forms.

This article categorizes each breaking change as _binary incompatible_ or _source incompatible_, or as a _behavioral change_:

*   **Binary incompatible** - When run against the new runtime or component, existing binaries may encounter a breaking change in behavior, such as failure to load or execute, and if so, require recompilation.
    
*   **Source incompatible** - When recompiled using the new SDK or component or to target the new runtime, existing source code may require source changes to compile successfully.
    
*   **Behavioral change** - Existing code and binaries may behave differently at runtime. If the new behavior is undesirable, existing code would need to be updated and recompiled.
    

Note

This article is a work in progress. It's not a complete list of breaking changes in .NET 10.

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/10.0:
If you're migrating an app to .NET 10, the breaking changes listed here might affect you. Changes are grouped by technology area, such as ASP.NET Core or Windows Forms.

This article categorizes each breaking change as _binary incompatible_ or _source incompatible_, or as a _behavioral change_:

*   **Binary incompatible** - When run against the new runtime or component, existing binaries may encounter a breaking change in behavior, such as failure to load or execute, and if so, require recompilation.
    
*   **Source incompatible** - When recompiled using the new SDK or component or to target the new runtime, existing source code may require source changes to compile successfully.
    
*   **Behavioral change** - Existing code and binaries may behave differently at runtime. If the new behavior is undesirable, existing code would need to be updated and recompiled.
    

Note

This article is a work in progress. It's not a complete list of breaking changes in .NET 10.

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/core-libraries/10.0/obsolete-apis:
Some APIs have been marked as obsolete, starting in .NET 10. This breaking change is specific to APIs that have been marked as obsolete _with a custom diagnostic ID_. Suppressing the default obsoletion diagnostic ID, which is [CS0618](../../../../csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0618) for the C# compiler, does not suppress the warnings that the compiler generates when these APIs are used.

## Change description

In previous .NET versions, these APIs can be used without any build warning. In .NET 10 and later versions, use of these APIs produces a compile-time warning or error with a custom diagnostic ID. The use of custom diagnostic IDs allows you to suppress the obsoletion warnings individually instead of blanket-suppressing all obsoletion warnings.

The following table lists the custom diagnostic IDs and their corresponding warning messages for obsoleted APIs.

Diagnostic ID

Description

Severity

[SYSLIB0058](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0058)

The `KeyExchangeAlgorithm`, `KeyExchangeStrength`, `CipherAlgorithm`, `CipherAlgorithmStrength`, `HashAlgorithm`, and `HashStrength` properties of [SslStream](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream) are obsolete. Use [NegotiatedCipherSuite](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.negotiatedciphersuite#system-net-security-sslstream-negotiatedciphersuite) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0059](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0059)

[SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown](/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.win32.systemevents.eventsthreadshutdown#microsoft-win32-systemevents-eventsthreadshutdown) callbacks aren't run before the process exits. Use [AppDomain.ProcessExit](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.appdomain.processexit#system-appdomain-processexit) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0060](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0060)

[System.Security.Cryptography.Rfc2898DeriveBytes](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes) constructors are obsolete. Use [Rfc2898DeriveBytes.Pbkdf2](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes.pbkdf2) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0061](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0061)

[Queryable.MaxBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.maxby#system-linq-queryable-maxby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\)) and [Queryable.MinBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.minby#system-linq-queryable-minby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\)) taking an `IComparer<TSource>` are obsolete. Use the new ones that take an `IComparer<TKey>`.

Warning

[SYSLIB0062](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0062)

[XsltSettings.EnableScript](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.xml.xsl.xsltsettings.enablescript#system-xml-xsl-xsltsettings-enablescript) is obsolete.

Warning

## Version introduced

.NET 10

## Type of breaking change

These obsoletions can affect [source compatibility](../../categories#source-compatibility).

## Recommended action

*   Follow the specific guidance provided for the each diagnostic ID using the URL link provided on the warning.
    
*   Warnings or errors for these obsoletions can't be suppressed using the standard diagnostic ID for obsolete types or members; use the custom `SYSLIBxxxx` diagnostic ID value instead.
    

## Affected APIs

### SYSLIB0058

*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.keyexchangealgorithm#system-net-security-sslstream-keyexchangealgorithm)
*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.KeyExchangeStrength](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.keyexchangestrength#system-net-security-sslstream-keyexchangestrength)
*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.CipherAlgorithm](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.cipheralgorithm#system-net-security-sslstream-cipheralgorithm)
*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.CipherStrength](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.cipherstrength#system-net-security-sslstream-cipherstrength)
*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.HashAlgorithm](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.hashalgorithm#system-net-security-sslstream-hashalgorithm)
*   [System.Net.Security.SslStream.HashStrength](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.hashstrength#system-net-security-sslstream-hashstrength)
*   [System.Security.Authentication.ExchangeAlgorithmType](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.authentication.exchangealgorithmtype)
*   [System.Security.Authentication.CipherAlgorithmType](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.authentication.cipheralgorithmtype)
*   [System.Security.Authentication.HashAlgorithmType](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.authentication.hashalgorithmtype)

### SYSLIB0059

*   [Microsoft.Win32.SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown](/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.win32.systemevents.eventsthreadshutdown#microsoft-win32-systemevents-eventsthreadshutdown)

### SYSLIB0060

*   [System.Security.Cryptography.Rfc2898DeriveBytes](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes)
*   [System.Security.Cryptography.Rfc2898DeriveBytes.Pbkdf2](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.security.cryptography.rfc2898derivebytes.pbkdf2)

### SYSLIB0061

*   [System.Linq.Queryable.MaxBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.maxby#system-linq-queryable-maxby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\))
*   [System.Linq.Queryable.MinBy<TSource,TKey>(IQueryable<TSource>, Expression<Func<TSource,TKey>>, IComparer<TSource>)](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.linq.queryable.minby#system-linq-queryable-minby-2\(system-linq-iqueryable\(\(-0\)\)-system-linq-expressions-expression\(\(system-func\(\(-0-1\)\)\)\)-system-collections-generic-icomparer\(\(-0\)\)\))

### SYSLIB0062

*   [System.Xml.Xsl.XsltSettings.EnableScript](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.xml.xsl.xsltsettings.enablescript#system-xml-xsl-xsltsettings-enablescript)

## See also

*   [API obsoletions with non-default diagnostic IDs (.NET 9)](../9.0/obsolete-apis-with-custom-diagnostics)
*   [API obsoletions with non-default diagnostic IDs (.NET 8)](../8.0/obsolete-apis-with-custom-diagnostics)
*   [Obsolete features in .NET 5+](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/obsoletions-overview)

Contents of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/core-libraries/10.0/obsolete-apis:
Some APIs have been marked as obsolete, starting in .NET 10. This breaking change is specific to APIs that have been marked as obsolete _with a custom diagnostic ID_. Suppressing the default obsoletion diagnostic ID, which is [CS0618](../../../../csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0618) for the C# compiler, does not suppress the warnings that the compiler generates when these APIs are used.

## Change description

In previous .NET versions, these APIs can be used without any build warning. In .NET 10 and later versions, use of these APIs produces a compile-time warning or error with a custom diagnostic ID. The use of custom diagnostic IDs allows you to suppress the obsoletion warnings individually instead of blanket-suppressing all obsoletion warnings.

The following table lists the custom diagnostic IDs and their corresponding warning messages for obsoleted APIs.

Diagnostic ID

Description

Severity

[SYSLIB0058](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0058)

Use [NegotiatedCipherSuite](/en-us/dotnet/api/system.net.security.sslstream.negotiatedciphersuite#system-net-security-sslstream-negotiatedciphersuite) instead.

Warning

[SYSLIB0059](../../../../fundamentals/syslib-diagnostics/syslib0059)

All four areas of your code will produce **compile-time warnings** in .NET 10:

| Diagnostic | Affected APIs | Recommended Fix |
|---|---|---|
| **SYSLIB0058** | `SslStream.KeyExchangeAlgorithm`, `.CipherAlgorithm`, `.HashAlgorithm`, and all `*Strength` properties | Use `SslStream.NegotiatedCipherSuite` instead |
| **SYSLIB0059** | `SystemEvents.EventsThreadShutdown` | Use `AppDomain.ProcessExit` — the old callbacks aren't actually run before process exit |
| **SYSLIB0061** | `Queryable.MaxBy` with `IComparer<TSource>` | Switch to the new overload that takes `IComparer<TKey>` (compares on the key, not the source) |
| **SYSLIB0062** | `XsltSettings.EnableScript` | XSLT script execution is being phased out; review whether you can eliminate script blocks |

All four are **warnings**, not errors, so your code will still compile. You can suppress them individually by diagnostic ID (e.g., `#pragma warning disable SYSLIB0058`) — the standard `CS0618` suppression won't work for these.
